copilot.all_clear_no_actions: All clear — no actions suggested right now.
copilot.ask_intro: Co-pilot grounds every answer in your actual grants, applications, and policies — with citations.
chart.ai_caption_unavailable: AI caption unavailable — chart data still above.
tour.donor.s1.title: Your portfolio command center
tour.donor.s1.body: Today's verdict synthesizes the 3-5 things that need your attention this week — AI-derived from your actual grants, applications, and risk signals.
tour.donor.s2.title: Charts with AI insight captions
tour.donor.s2.body: Every chart has a "so what" caption written by AI. Scan a dashboard in 10 seconds, not 10 minutes.
tour.donor.s3.title: Co-pilot is always a click away
tour.donor.s3.body: Tap the sparkle on the right edge anytime. Ask Co-pilot about portfolio health, write a new RFP, or scan for patterns across declined applications.
tour.donor.s4.title: Design grants with AI
tour.donor.s4.body: Under "Create grant," Kuja suggests eligibility criteria, rubric weights, and reporting requirements tuned to your goal + thematic + geography.
tour.ngo.s1.title: Your readiness score
tour.ngo.s1.body: A 0–100 signal of how competitive your next application is likely to be. AI updates this as your documents, applications, and capacity change.
tour.ngo.s2.title: AI-coached next actions
tour.ngo.s2.body: The three actions here are ranked by impact on your readiness score — with estimated uplift per action.
tour.ngo.s3.title: Browse grants smart
tour.ngo.s3.body: Every grant shows your AI-estimated fit + win probability + what's missing from your profile. Focus where you'll win.
tour.ngo.s4.title: Co-pilot writes with you
tour.ngo.s4.body: Stuck on a section? Tap the sparkle. Co-pilot grounds every suggestion in your own past applications — never generic.
tour.reviewer.s1.title: AI-prioritized queue
tour.reviewer.s1.body: Your assignments are ranked by review priority — deadline, complexity, and similarity to prior high-scoring apps.
tour.reviewer.s2.title: Compare mode
tour.reviewer.s2.body: Select 2–5 applications to compare side-by-side. AI aligns strengths, weaknesses, and flags potentially coordinated submissions.
tour.reviewer.s3.title: Justify every score
tour.reviewer.s3.body: AI pre-fills rationale per rubric criterion using application content. You edit and confirm — much faster than writing cold.
tour.admin.s1.title: Operations at a glance
tour.admin.s1.body: Anomaly stream flags unusual patterns — spikes in declined apps, slow review queues, suspicious verification results.
tour.admin.s2.title: Live AI health panel
tour.admin.s2.body: Every AI call across the platform is logged — success rate, tokens, per-endpoint breakdowns. Catch regressions before users notice.
tour.admin.s3.title: Configure and trust
tour.admin.s3.body: Compliance trust panel, audit timelines, typed-confirmation on destructive actions — this is the platform a board can trust to run a fund.
report.precheck_subtitle: Let's check your draft against the donor's expectations before you submit — better to fix gaps now than face a revision request later.

observability.title: AI observability
observability.subtitle: Health, throughput, latency, and anomalies across every AI endpoint
observability.anomalies_none: No anomalies in the last 24 hours.

glossary.readiness_score: A 0-100 score of how competitive your next grant application is likely to be. Updated automatically as your documents, applications, and capacity change.
glossary.capacity_assessment: A structured self-assessment using a recognized framework (Kuja, STEP, UN-HACT, CHS, NUPAS) that scores your organization across capacity, governance, finance, and programs. Donors weight this when reviewing applications.
glossary.eligibility: The conditions your organization must meet before applying — country, sector, registration, budget size, etc. The donor sets these.
glossary.rubric: The scoring framework reviewers use. Each criterion has a weight; the weighted total becomes your application score.
glossary.compliance: Continuous checks: sanctions screening (UN/OFAC/EU), government registry verification, and grant reporting status. Donors see a green/amber/red posture per grantee.
assessment.results.headline_good: You’re ready to apply with confidence
assessment.results.headline_starting: Let’s work on key foundations together
assessment.results.subhead_excellent: Your capacity scores in the top tier. Apply to ambitious multi-year grants and lead consortia with confidence.
assessment.results.subhead_good: You’re competitive for most grants. Focus on the lower-scoring areas to unlock larger awards.
assessment.results.subhead_developing: You meet the basics for many grants. Strengthen the weakest dimensions to reach the next tier.
assessment.results.subhead_starting: Every strong NGO started here. The action steps below show your highest-leverage next moves.
assessment.results.unlocks_excellent: Multi-year programmatic grants · Consortium leadership · Prime contractor roles
assessment.results.unlocks_good: Single-year programmatic grants · Sub-grants from intermediaries · Most thematic calls
assessment.results.unlocks_developing: Pilot grants · Small grants programs · Capacity-building grants
assessment.results.unlocks_starting: Capacity-building grants · Mentor partnerships · Foundation seed grants
applications.detail.summary_subtitle_dra: Draft — not yet submitted to the donor.
org.profile_completion_subtitle: A complete profile makes AI suggestions more accurate and your applications stronger.
org.complete_profile_cta: Fill the gaps below to reach 100%
verification.intro_body: Green: registry-confirmed and trustworthy. Amber: AI-parsed with high confidence — a human should still confirm. Red: flagged or unverified — review before proceeding.
glossary.application_status: Where this application sits in the funder’s pipeline. Draft = not submitted; Submitted = waiting for assignment; Under review = a reviewer is scoring; Scored = awaiting funding decision; Awarded/Rejected = final.
framework.kuja.label: Kuja Framework
framework.kuja.description: Kuja Link standard capacity assessment covering 5 key organizational domains.
framework.step.label: STEP Framework
framework.step.description: Strengthening Effective Partner Engagement and Performance assessment used by major international NGOs.
framework.un_hact.label: UN-HACT
framework.un_hact.description: UN Harmonized Approach to Cash Transfers micro-assessment for implementing partners.
framework.chs.label: CHS (Core Humanitarian Standard)
framework.chs.description: Core Humanitarian Standard on Quality and Accountability self-assessment.
framework.nupas.label: NUPAS
framework.nupas.description: Non-profit Unified Performance Assessment System for comprehensive organizational evaluation.

compliance_copilot.intro: Get a plain-language read on this organization’s verification + sanctions findings, with concrete follow-up actions.
donor.scaffold.burden_explainer: Estimated time + complexity for an NGO to apply. Lower burden attracts more applicants and reduces incomplete submissions.

coauthor.heading: AI co-author
coauthor.subtitle: Type a short brief in any language. We'll use your org profile, prior applications, and uploaded documents to generate a complete first draft of every section. Edit anything before submitting.
coauthor.brief_placeholder: e.g. We want to focus on female-led smallholder farms in Kakamega using SMS-based agronomy coaching, drawing on our 2024 maize program.
coauthor.applied_to_form: Applied to your form — review and edit
coauthor.preview_only: Preview only — start an application to apply
grant_brief.cta.open: Or design with AI from a prompt
grant_brief.heading: Design from a prompt
grant_brief.subtitle: Type 1-2 lines about who you want to fund and what outcome you care about. AI will design a complete first-draft grant — title, description, criteria with weights, eligibility, documents, reporting cadence, deadline. Edit before publishing.
grant_brief.placeholder: e.g. $500k for women-led climate adaptation in coastal Kenya, focus on smallholder agroforestry, 18-month duration
grant_brief.toast.applied: Grant designed: {criteria} criteria · review and edit
ai.confidence.high.tooltip: High — claim is directly supported by a named source we can show you.
ai.confidence.medium.tooltip: Medium — claim is reasonable based on your sources but check the specifics.
ai.confidence.low.tooltip: Low — generic claim or extrapolation; verify before submitting.
provenance.empty: No source citations recorded for this section.
matches.heading: Best matches for you
matches.subtitle: Open grants ranked by your win probability
matches.empty.title: No matches yet
matches.empty.body: We'll surface ranked grants once we've scored you against the open call list.
report_coauthor.heading: AI report draft
report_coauthor.subtitle: Add a few notes about what happened this period — meetings, milestones, beneficiary numbers. AI will draft every section grounded in your prior reports + uploaded documents, and flag any gaps that need evidence.
report_coauthor.notes_placeholder: e.g. We trained 60 new CHWs in Q3 across 3 counties. Attendance sheets uploaded. Two scheduled trainings were postponed due to election logistics.
grant_qa.heading: Questions about this grant
grant_qa.ngo_subtitle: Ask the donor a question. Once they answer, the answer becomes visible to every NGO applying.
grant_qa.donor_subtitle: Answers you give are visible to every NGO applying to this grant.
grant_qa.answer_placeholder: Write your answer — it will be visible to every applicant.
grant_qa.ask_placeholder: Ask the donor anything you'd like clarified before submitting.
grant_qa.confirm_moderate: Hide this question from the public Q&A? It will remain in the audit log.
grant_qa.toast.answered: Answer published — visible to all applicants
observability.helpfulness.heading: AI helpfulness signal
observability.helpfulness.empty: No AI calls in this window.
observability.helpfulness.footnote: Helpful % weights 'used' as 1.0, 'edited' as 0.5, 'dismissed' as 0. Calls without a feedback signal are excluded from the percentage.
drafters.label: drafting now
drafters.tooltip: Active drafts in the last {days} days
application.activity.heading: Activity timeline
application.activity.subtitle: Every event on this application — state changes, AI runs, reviewer touches, document uploads.
application.activity.coming_soon: The activity timeline is being prepared for your account.
preview_reviewer.heading: How the reviewer will see this
preview_reviewer.subtitle: This is what the donor's reviewer sees when scoring your application — same layout, same per-criterion order, same word counts. Use this to spot thin responses before submitting.
preview_reviewer.empty_response: No response yet — the reviewer will see this section as empty.
preview_reviewer.thin_warning: {n} response(s) are thin — they'll likely score lower with the reviewer. Consider adding specific examples, numbers, or evidence before submitting.
readiness.title: Submission readiness
readiness.scanning: Scanning your application for gaps, weak answers, and overclaims…
readiness.fallback_notice: AI pre-flight is offline — we ran a basic check. Re-run when AI is available.
readiness.gaps_subtitle: Sorted by severity. Blockers must be fixed before submitting.
readiness.applied_toast: Rewrite applied — review the change before submitting.
report_readiness.title: Report readiness
report_readiness.scanning: Scanning your report from a donor's perspective…
median_preview.heading: Predicted applicant pool
median_preview.subtitle: AI imagines what a median qualifying NGO will submit and rates how well each criterion will discriminate between strong and weak applicants.
score_breakdown.tooltip: Click to see how this score breaks down
score_breakdown.empty: No component data available for this score.
portfolio.heading: Portfolio diagnostics
portfolio.subtitle: Cross-grant patterns to help you tighten criteria and read your applicant pool.
portfolio.anomaly.low_interest: Only {n} submission(s). Consider broader sectors, lighter eligibility, or longer deadline.
portfolio.anomaly.low_discrimination: Score spread of just {spread} pts across applicants — criteria aren't separating winners from the pool. Tighten the highest-weight criterion.
portfolio.anomaly.criteria_too_easy: Average AI score is {avg}% — criteria may be too easy. Add specificity to keep the bar high.
portfolio.anomaly.high_decline: {pct}% of applicants are being declined — eligibility may be too narrow or criteria misaligned.
reviewer_summary.title: AI summary
reviewer_summary.rationale_used: Rationale pasted into the highest-weighted criterion
reviewer_summary.editable_note: Edit before submitting — your judgment is the final word.
burden.title: Applicant burden + design check
burden.recommend_extend: Consider extending the deadline by {n} day(s) given the burden.
burden.too_burdensome: Asks that lock out smaller NGOs
this_week.title: This week
this_week.empty: You're all caught up — nothing flagged for this week.
status.ngo.under_review: A reviewer is reading your application now
status.ngo.rejected: Not awarded this round — feedback coming soon
status.ngo.draft: In draft — keep going when you can
decision_audit.title: Decision audit
decision_audit.ai_summary_note: Counts are AI invocations, not suggestions accepted. Heuristic only.
org_memory.title: Organizational memory
org_memory.subtitle: Reusable facts, narratives, partners, and evidence the AI co-author pulls from when drafting your applications and reports.
org_memory.empty: No memory items yet. Add one above, or submit an application — we extract facts automatically.
org_memory.disabled_title: Org memory is not enabled for your organization yet.
org_memory.disabled_body: Once enabled, the AI co-author will reuse your prior facts, narratives, and partner descriptions automatically.
org_memory.field.label: Short label (e.g. "Beneficiaries 2024")
org_memory.field.content_placeholder: The fact, narrative, evidence excerpt, or partner description to remember…
